summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: How Order.co is modernizing procurement and spend management at scale
articles.description: How Order.co automates procurement at scale with virtual cards and spend controls.

articles.title: Card volume rises with digital boost
articles.description: Volume on Visa, Mastercard, American Express and Discover cards issued in the U.S. climbed 6.4% last year over 2024, with an assist from digital plays, according to Nilson Report’s publisher.

articles.title: Walmart alumni open POS test lab
articles.description: As retailers seek omnichannel sales solutions, a new vendor-neutral demonstration lab offers point-of-sale equipment evaluation.

articles.title: Stablecoins roll on as rules evolve
articles.description: PayPal, Convera and Nium are among the companies racing to incorporate stablecoins in cross-border plays, even as regulators voice caution.

articles.title: Sharpton cites BNPL racial disparities
articles.description: The civil rights leader noted that Black and Hispanic consumers are more likely to use buy now, pay later loans and urged state AGs to expand their inquiry.

articles.title: Branch CFO says IPOs are a ‘discipline, not a deadline’
articles.description: Matt Peterson, who joined the fintech as finance chief in late 2025, previously led an initial public offering for cloud computing services company Fastly.

articles.title: How TreviPay’s CFO starts his day
articles.description: Chief Financial Officer Joel Campbell shares insights on consistency, handling disruptions and the James Bond item on his desk.

articles.title: Block’s Dorsey rethinks corporate structure
articles.description: As AI plays a bigger role in operations, the middle management layer becomes less important, the company’s head says.
